evals.prompt: Define an ElectroDB entity called Order with an orderId, customerId, status, and createdAt. I need to be able to query all orders for a given customer, so add a secondary index for that access pattern.
evals.expected_output: A TypeScript entity using ElectroDB's Entity constructor with a model block, typed attributes, a primary table index keyed on orderId, and a GSI keyed on customerId so orders can be queried per customer.
assertions: imports and uses `new Entity` from the `electrodb` package
declares a `model` block with `entity`, `service`, and `version` fields
defines a primary index plus a separate GSI that carries an `index` property
all index pk and sk definitions use `composite` arrays
the GSI partition key composite includes `customerId`

evals.prompt: Using my single-table-design ElectroDB setup, write a query that fetches every task for project 'proj-x' assigned to employees whose id starts with 'emp-'.
evals.expected_output: An ElectroDB query that selects the access pattern (index) by its logical name, supplies the partition-key composite, narrows the sort key with a begins_with operation, and executes with .go().
assertions: calls `query.<indexName>(...)` to select the access pattern by logical name
passes the partition-key composite (the project id) as an object argument
uses `.begins({ ... })` to filter the sort key by prefix

evals.prompt: Write an ElectroDB conditional update that sets an order's status to 'shipped' only if its current status is 'paid'.
evals.expected_output: An ElectroDB update call that targets the item by key, sets the new status, and attaches a condition that the existing status equals 'paid' so the write fails when the condition is not met.
assertions: uses `.update({ ... })` keyed by the item's identifiers
uses `.set({ ... })` to assign the new status value
uses `.where((attr, op) => ...)` to express the conditional check
the condition compares the status attribute with `op.eq` against 'paid'

evals.prompt: Show me how to page through ElectroDB query results 25 at a time, including how to fetch the next page.
evals.expected_output: An ElectroDB query executed with a limit option that destructures the returned cursor, then a follow-up query that passes that cursor back in to retrieve the next page.
assertions: feeds the saved `cursor` into a subsequent `.go({ cursor })` call
